reasoning: OpenAI announcing AGI by the end of 2025 is highly unlikely for multiple reasons. First, technological progress, while rapid, is not robustly predicted to reach full AGI (outperforming humans at most economically valuable work) by 2025. Sam Altman and other leaders have typically suggested timelines closer to 2027-2030. Second, explicitly declaring AGI has massive legal and financial implications for OpenAI, notably regarding their Microsoft partnership, which excludes AGI from IP licenses. This creates a strong disincentive to prematurely "announce" AGI. Finally, the market freeze value of 4.15% supports a low probability. Although unexpected breakthroughs could happen, the structural constraints make an official 2025 announcement highly improbable.
